bugs.error_message: SunfishDialog renders with browser defaults under Material provider (no surface, no elevation, no typography, no motion)
bugs.root_cause: Partial was a 5-line TODO stub — compiled sunfish-material.css contained zero .sf-dialog rules, so MaterialCssProvider.DialogClass() emission (sf-dialog / sf-dialog-overlay / sf-dialog-title / sf-dialog-body / sf-dialog-actions / sf-dialog-close) had nothing to bind to
bugs.fix: Authored full M3 dialog partial (297 lines): surface-container-high on corner-extra-large (28px), elevation-3, headline-small title / body-medium body / label-large actions, 24dp padding column flex with 24dp gap, 8dp action gap, 40x40 close icon-button with currentColor state layer (8/12/12), 3px outer secondary focus stroke with 2px offset, 32% scrim via overlay ::before (identical in dark — NOT bumped to 52%), enter keyframe scale(0.8)->1 + opacity 0->1 over duration-medium2 with easing-emphasized-decel, size modifiers .sf-dialog--small/medium/large/full applied directly to .sf-dialog root (NOT the non-existent .sf-dialog__panel the FluentUI skin targets), responsive full-screen below 600px. Mixins sf-type-role / sf-responsive-breakpoint inlined because foundation Sass variables do not cross @use boundaries in this repo.

bugs.error_message: SunfishCalendar renders with browser <table> defaults under Material provider (no surface, no cell shape, no state layers, no typography)
bugs.root_cause: Partial was a 5-line TODO stub — compiled sunfish-material.css contained zero .sf-calendar rules, so every class emitted by SunfishCalendar.razor (sf-calendar__cell and its 7 modifiers, __day-header, __nav-btn, __title, __grid, __year-grid / __decade-grid / __century-grid and their cell variants) fell back to user-agent defaults
bugs.fix: Authored full M3 date-picker partial (445 lines): surface-container-high on corner-extra-large container, circular 40x40 day cells (32x32 under [data-sf-density=compact]) with currentColor state-layer ::before at 8/10/12 opacity (focus overridden to 10% per M3 spec rather than global 12%), selected cells primary/on-primary, today 1px primary inset ring unselected, .sf-calendar__cell--in-range selector pre-wired with secondary-container fill (razor does not yet emit — flagged for Theme 1 follow-up), other-month and disabled at 38% opacity, body-large day numbers / label-medium day-of-week / title-small header button / label-small week-number column (Sunfish extension flagged in comment), 40x40 circular nav chevrons, 3px secondary outer-stroke focus rings with 2px offset on every interactive cell, year view 3-col grid / decade + century views 4-col grid with pill-shaped buttons sharing the same state-layer + selected/current semantics. Typography inlined rather than via sf-type-role mixin for the same Sass scope reason as bug-101.

bugs.root_cause: background: rgba($primary, 0.12) → $primary !important; color: $text-muted → $white !important; hover: not(:disabled) { → not(.sf-calendar__cell--disabled) .sf-calendar__day {
bugs.fix: Changed background: rgba($primary, 0.12) → $primary !important; color: $text-muted → $white !important; hover: not(:disabled) { → not(.sf-calendar__cell--disabled) .sf-calendar__day {

bugs.root_cause: border: none → 0; padding: 0 4px → 0; color: inherit → var(--sf-color-primary, $primary)
bugs.fix: Changed border: none → 0; padding: 0 4px → 0; color: inherit → var(--sf-color-primary, $primary)

bugs.error_message: Fluent skin rendered Fabric-flavored instead of Fluent v9 — wrong surface colors, too-bold selection, hardcoded button hover/active hexes broke dark mode
bugs.root_cause: --sf-color-primary was Fluent v8 (Fabric) #0078d4 instead of Fluent v9 colorBrandBackground #0f6cbd; no v9 token shim existed so v9-only tokens (colorNeutralBackground1/2/3, colorStrokeFocus2, colorSubtleBackgroundHover, shadow16, spacingHorizontal*, strokeWidth*, borderRadius*, full typescale) were missing. Button-Fluent danger/warning/info/success hover/active hardcoded Fabric-era hex (#c43035, #e69a3e, #00a8d6, #0e6e0e, #a52b2f, #cc8a38, #0094bd, #0c5e0c) bypassing the token system, so dark mode left them as light-theme reds/greens.
bugs.fix: Shipped fluent-v9-tokens.css (537 lines) at packages/ui-adapters-blazor/Providers/FluentUI/wwwroot/css/fluent-v9-tokens.css emitting the full Fluent v9 token ladder on :root and [data-sf-theme=dark] (neutral/brand/status color families with Background1/2/3 + Foreground1/2/3, typescale fontSizeBase100-1000 + lineHeights + weights, spacingHorizontal/Vertical None-XXXL, strokeWidth Thin/Thick/Thicker/Thickest, borderRadius None-Circular, shadow2/4/8/16/28/64 + shadowBrand variants, durationUltraFast-UltraSlow, curves). Rewired foundation/_colors.scss so every --sf-color-* reads via var(--colorXxx, fabric-fallback) — e.g. --sf-color-primary: var(--colorBrandBackground, #0f6cbd). Added --sf-color-{danger|warning|info|success}-{hover|active} routed through colorStatus*Foreground1/2 (light + dark override). Retrofitted components/_button.scss to replace the 8 hardcoded button hex values with those new hover/active tokens and added border-color on hover/active. Wired fluent-v9-tokens.css into apps/kitchen-sink/App.razor ahead of sunfish-fluentui.css and extended the provider-toggle script to disable the tokens link when switching providers. Context7 MCP quota was exhausted so token values were sourced from @fluentui/tokens on GitHub (microsoft/fluentui/tree/master/packages/tokens/src).

bugs.error_message: Calendar cells in Bootstrap skin rendered with user-agent defaults (no today/selected/in-range visuals); range mode never highlighted endpoints or span
bugs.root_cause: SCSS styled a non-existent .sf-calendar__day--today / --selected / --in-range element, but Razor emits the modifier on the .sf-calendar__cell <td> and nests a plain <span class='sf-calendar__day'> inside. Result: the rules selected nothing. Razor also never emitted range-start, range-end, or in-range classes so there were no hooks for a range visual even with correct selectors.
bugs.fix: Rewrote the dead .sf-calendar__day--* block to the cell-descendant pattern (.sf-calendar__cell { &--today .sf-calendar__day {...} &--selected .sf-calendar__day {...} &--range-start/--range-end/--range-edge .sf-calendar__day {...} &--in-range { background: rgba($primary,0.12); .sf-calendar__day { border-radius: 0; } } }) and appended conditional emissions in SunfishCalendar.razor for isRangeStart / isRangeEnd / isInRange.

bugs.error_message: Calendar in Fluent skin rendered with user-agent defaults — no header, no weekday headers, no cell visuals
bugs.root_cause: Fluent SCSS used single-hyphen class names (.sf-calendar-header, .sf-calendar-nav-btn, .sf-calendar-title, .sf-calendar-grid, .sf-calendar-day-*) but Razor emits BEM double-underscore (.sf-calendar__header, .sf-calendar__nav-btn, .sf-calendar__title, .sf-calendar__grid, .sf-calendar__cell, .sf-calendar__day). Every rule selected nothing.
bugs.fix: Rewrote the .sf-calendar-* block as .sf-calendar__* using BEM double-underscore. Added the full modifier ladder — &--today, &--selected, &--range-start, &--range-end, &--range-edge, &--in-range, &--focused, &--other-month, &--disabled, &--empty — using the cell-descendant pattern so .sf-calendar__day inside .sf-calendar__cell gets styled correctly.

bugs.error_message: Dialog title was not draggable in Fluent skin even with Draggable=true; size modifiers (Small/Medium/Large/Full) had no visual effect
bugs.root_cause: Two dead-CSS bugs in one file. (a) .sf-dialog--draggable nested rules targeted .sf-dialog-header but Razor emits .sf-dialog-title — drag-cursor rule selected nothing. (b) Size selectors targeted .sf-dialog--small .sf-dialog__panel / --medium / --large / --full but Razor never emits a .sf-dialog__panel descendant; size modifier classes attach directly to the .sf-dialog root.
bugs.fix: Re-targeted the draggable cursor rule to .sf-dialog--draggable .sf-dialog-title (and :active → grabbing). Moved size-modifier rules onto the root: .sf-dialog--small/--medium/--large { max-width: var(--sf-dialog-{sm|md|lg}-max-width, 400|600|900px); } and .sf-dialog--full { top:0; left:0; transform:none; max-width:100%; width:100%; height:100%; border-radius:0; }.

bugs.error_message: Bootstrap DataGrid rendered as unstyled rows/cells — no border, no stripes, no hover — because the inner <table> was not receiving Bootstrap .table semantics
bugs.root_cause: The inner <table> element emitted by the Razor tree carries only .sf-bs-datagrid-table (or a grid container class) — it does not carry the .table class that Bootstrap's _tables.scss keys every rule off of (Bootstrap's table styles are scoped as .table > tr > td { --bs-table-color-state: ... } via CSS custom properties). Without .table, none of the BS table tokens (--bs-table-color, --bs-table-bg, --bs-table-border-color, --bs-table-hover-bg) applied. Adding a new ISunfishCssProvider.DataGridTableClass() method was out of scope for this pass.
bugs.fix: CSS-only shim: declare the Bootstrap table tokens on .sf-bs-datagrid (--bs-table-color, --bs-table-bg, --bs-table-border-color, --bs-table-hover-bg with theme-aware fallbacks) and add `> tbody > tr:hover > *` hover-row rule. This delivers the .table visuals without touching the Razor tree or the provider contract. Adding a DataGridTableClass() provider method is deferred.

bugs.error_message: DataGrid command buttons, column-menu trigger, and loading spinner had no visuals in Bootstrap skin — legacy marilo-era class names carried over but no CSS ever defined them
bugs.root_cause: Razor still emits three marilo-legacy hooks — .mar-datagrid-cmd-btn (and variants --sm, --delete, --add), .mar-datagrid-column-menu-trigger, and .sf-datagrid-loading-spinner — but no Bootstrap provider rule matched any of them. The spinner in particular had no @keyframes so it rendered as a static character.
bugs.fix: Appended a scoped block under .sf-bs-datagrid styling all three: .mar-datagrid-cmd-btn with size and role variants (--sm, --delete, --add), .mar-datagrid-column-menu-trigger as an icon button, and .sf-datagrid-loading-spinner with a fresh @keyframes sf-bs-datagrid-spin (0→360deg, 0.9s linear infinite). All values route through Bootstrap tokens (--bs-btn-*, --bs-danger-rgb, --bs-success-rgb, --bs-emphasis-color-rgb) so dark mode inherits automatically.

bugs.error_message: Dialog Size parameter had no visual effect in Bootstrap skin — Small/Medium/Large/Full dialogs all rendered at default width
bugs.root_cause: Bootstrap 5 sizes modal width via --bs-modal-width on .modal-dialog, but no Sunfish size modifiers were wired to override that token. Adding a Size parameter to the Razor component and emitting .sf-dialog--small/--medium/--large/--full from the provider was out of scope for this pass; all we had to work with was the CSS class a consumer might attach via CssClass.
bugs.fix: Added narrowly-scoped rules on .modal-dialog, .sf-bs-dialog--draggable that respond to .sf-dialog--small/--medium/--large/--full when consumers attach them via CssClass: { &.sf-dialog--small { --bs-modal-width: 400px; } &.sf-dialog--medium { --bs-modal-width: 600px; } &.sf-dialog--large { --bs-modal-width: 900px; } &.sf-dialog--full { max-width:100%; width:100%; height:100%; margin:0; .modal-content { border-radius:0; min-height:100vh; } } }. Adding the Size parameter to SunfishDialog.razor + per-provider class emission is deferred.

bugs.error_message: Icon buttons in Bootstrap skin rendered as wide pill-shaped buttons instead of square icon targets
bugs.root_cause: BootstrapCssProvider.IconButtonClass() emits `btn btn-icon sf-bs-icon-button` plus optional `btn-sm` / `btn-lg`, but no rule overrode Bootstrap's default .btn horizontal padding (--bs-btn-padding-x: 0.75rem). The existing .sf-bs-icon-button rule was never strong enough to beat .btn in specificity, so the padding stayed rectangular and no aspect-ratio was applied — the element rendered as a wide pill.
bugs.fix: Added a compound-selector block (.btn.sf-bs-icon-button, .btn-icon.sf-bs-icon-button, plus two sibling hooks used by the SignalR row and datagrid pager: .sf-signalr-row__action.sf-bs-icon-button, .sf-bs-datagrid-pager-btn.sf-bs-icon-button) that sets --bs-btn-padding-x/y to 0.5rem, aspect-ratio: 1/1, and inline-flex centering. Added .btn-sm.sf-bs-icon-button (0.375rem) and .btn-lg.sf-bs-icon-button (0.625rem) to cover all three size tiers.

bugs.error_message: SunfishDialog opens without focus trap / Esc close / body scroll-lock / focus restoration — WCAG 2.1.2 + screen-reader navigation regression
bugs.root_cause: The dialog was rendered with @if (Visible) { ... } alone — no lifecycle hook managed focus, no JS handled Tab cycling or Esc, and nested dialogs could leave <body> permanently scroll-locked because nothing reference-counted an overflow-hidden class. Tab escaped the modal to background content; Esc was a no-op; page scrolled behind the dialog; focus was lost to <body> when the dialog closed.
bugs.fix: (1) Added sunfish-dialog-a11y.js ES module under packages/ui-adapters-blazor/wwwroot/js/, loaded lazily via IJSRuntime.InvokeAsync<IJSObjectReference>("import", ...) matching the existing Sunfish convention (sunfish-positioning.js, sunfish-dragdrop.js). (2) Exposes attach(dialogId, dialogEl, dotNetRef, {closeOnEsc, escMethod}) and detach(dialogId). Captures document.activeElement on attach, moves focus to first focusable descendant, traps Tab / Shift+Tab by redirecting at boundaries, and restores focus to the captured element on detach. (3) Adds / removes body.sf-dialog-open class with a module-scoped reference count so nested dialogs compose — only the last detach removes the class. (4) SunfishDialog.razor now @implements IAsyncDisposable, @injects IJSRuntime, holds a _dialogRef ElementReference + _dialogId (stable Guid), binds tabindex=-1 on the root (for focus() fallback), wires OnAfterRenderAsync to attach/detach on Visible transitions, exposes [JSInvokable] OnEscFromJs, and disposes both the module and DotNetObjectReference. New [Parameter] public bool CloseOnEsc = true. (5) Three provider dialog partials (_dialog.scss × Bootstrap/FluentUI/Material) got `body.sf-dialog-open { overflow: hidden; }` so the class actually locks scroll.

bugs.error_message: SunfishCalendar used role="application" on outer wrapper — suppressed screen-reader virtual-cursor navigation across the month grid
bugs.root_cause: role="application" instructs screen readers to abandon virtual-cursor mode and forward every key event raw to the widget. That is only appropriate for fully custom keyboard interfaces (e.g., a CAD canvas) — not a date picker, whose expected interaction is the WAI-ARIA Grid pattern (arrow keys, Home/End, Page Up/Down). NVDA / JAWS users reported they could not arrow across the day grid because virtual cursor was disabled.
bugs.fix: Removed role="application" from the outer .sf-calendar div. The inner <table class="sf-calendar__grid"> already had role="grid"; added aria-rowcount (weeks.Count + 1 to include the header row), aria-colcount (7 or 8 depending on week-number column), aria-label bound to the current "MMMM yyyy" display. Added role="row" to each <tr>, role="columnheader" scope="col" to each <th>, and role="rowheader" to the week-number <td>. Added aria-current="date" on the <td> where isToday so the today cell announces correctly to screen readers (replacement signal for the dropped application role's "active cell" semantic).

bugs.error_message: Dialog and Calendar skin partials ship zero @media (forced-colors: active) branches — Windows High Contrast users cannot perceive selection, focus, or surface edges
bugs.root_cause: All three provider skins styled dialog and calendar using --sf-color-* / --bs-* token references. In Windows forced-colors mode the browser overrides those to system Canvas/CanvasText — selected cells, range edges, and focus rings all collapse to the same flat color. WCAG 1.4.11 (non-text contrast) fails for every a11y-critical surface of both components in Windows HC.
bugs.fix: Appended @media (forced-colors: active) blocks to all six relevant partials: Bootstrap/FluentUI/Material × _dialog.scss + _date-picker.scss. Each block sets a 1px solid CanvasText border + Canvas background on the component surface, 2px Highlight outline on :focus-visible for the close button / day cell / nav chevron / title button, and (on selection states) background: Highlight + color: HighlightText + forced-color-adjust: none so the inverse-video pair survives the forced-colors clamp. Also added @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks that zero transition and animation on the interactive surfaces so vestibular-sensitive users don't see the scale-in / state-layer fade. Selectors in each file tailored to the actual classes emitted by the razor under that provider (verified file-by-file, not templated).

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: DataGrid <table> missing Bootstrap .table class; BS5 styling not applied (TIER-4-RE-AUDIT Item 1 PARTIAL)
bugs.root_cause: SunfishDataGrid.razor line 54 rendered <table style="..."> with no class attribute, so BootstrapCssProvider could not contribute the .table class required for BS5 table styling (striping, borders, responsive behavior).
bugs.fix: Added DataGridTableClass() to ISunfishCssProvider. Bootstrap returns "table"; Fluent/Material return string.Empty. SunfishDataGrid.razor now emits class="@($"{CssProvider.DataGridTableClass()} sf-datagrid-table".Trim())". Did not use CombineClasses because that would append the consumer's outer Class property onto the inner <table>.

bugs.error_message: DataGrid command buttons hardcoded mar-datagrid-cmd-btn; BS5 .btn/.btn-sm/.btn-outline-secondary never emitted (TIER-4-RE-AUDIT Item 2 PARTIAL)
bugs.root_cause: SunfishDataGrid.Rendering.cs and SunfishGridCommandButton.razor emitted literal "mar-datagrid-cmd-btn" strings. BootstrapCssProvider had no way to inject Bootstrap button vocabulary, so grid command buttons lacked .btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-secondary.
bugs.fix: Added DataGridCommandButtonClass() to ISunfishCssProvider. Bootstrap returns "btn btn-sm btn-outline-secondary mar-datagrid-cmd-btn" (keeps mar-* so existing SCSS still targets). Fluent/Material return "mar-datagrid-cmd-btn". Replaced three hardcoded call-sites in Rendering.cs via replace_all and updated SunfishGridCommandButton.razor.

bugs.error_message: Icon-only SunfishButton not square under Bootstrap 5; rectangular with default btn padding (TIER-4-RE-AUDIT Item 3 PARTIAL)
bugs.root_cause: SunfishButton with only an Icon prop (no ChildContent) rendered as a regular .btn with text padding. Under BS5 this produced a wide rectangular button instead of a 1:1 square icon button at sm/md/lg tiers.
bugs.fix: Added IsIconOnly computed property (Icon != null && ChildContent == null) and emitted .sf-button--icon-only class. Added .btn.sf-button--icon-only { aspect-ratio: 1/1; width:2.5rem; height:2.5rem; } plus &.btn-sm (2rem) and &.btn-lg (3rem) overrides, using --bs-btn-padding-x/y custom properties to tame BS5 default padding.

bugs.root_cause: sf-bs-datagrid-cell: focus, → focus-visible,; sf-bs-datagrid-header-cell: focus { → focus-visible {
bugs.fix: Changed sf-bs-datagrid-cell: focus, → focus-visible,; sf-bs-datagrid-header-cell: focus { → focus-visible {
Rewrote 8→7 lines (2 removed) | Also: outline: 2px solid var(--bs-focus-ring-color, $pri; outline-offset: 1px;

bugs.root_cause: sf-fab: focus-visible { outline: none → focus-visible { @include sf-focus-ring
bugs.fix: Changed sf-fab: focus-visible { outline: none → focus-visible { @include sf-focus-ring

bugs.root_cause: display: flex → block; justify-content: flex-end → center
bugs.fix: Changed display: flex → block; justify-content: flex-end → center

bugs.error_message: SunfishDialog close button rendered literal × text glyph; ignored framework icon conventions and theme colors (SYNTHESIS Theme 9, Phase 2B Item 1)
bugs.root_cause: SunfishDialog.razor line 175 hard-coded <button>×</button>. Fails on icon fonts, doesn't match Bootstrap's native .btn-close SVG, doesn't pick up Fluent's currentColor or Material's state-layer chrome.
bugs.fix: Added DialogCloseMarkup() and DialogCloseButtonClass() to ISunfishCssProvider. Bootstrap returns empty markup + 'btn-close' class (BS5 draws its own SVG via CSS background). Fluent returns an inline dismiss SVG + 'sf-fluent-dialog-close'. Material returns a close + 'sf-m3-dialog-close'. Razor now emits class='sf-dialog-close {provider-class}' and renders @((MarkupString)CssProvider.DialogCloseMarkup()). Added CSS for '.sf-fluent-dialog-close' (32x32 subtle button). Updated Stubs.cs and apps/kitchen-sink/Services/ProviderSwitcher.cs to avoid CS0535.

bugs.error_message: Single --sf-focus-ring CSS variable doesn't match Bootstrap/Fluent/Material focus idioms (SYNTHESIS Theme 5, Phase 2B Item 2)
bugs.root_cause: A single --sf-focus-ring variable was defined once as '2px bg + 2px primary' and reused across all skins. BS5 spec is box-shadow diffused glow via --bs-focus-ring-*; Fluent v9 is a double-stroke inset using colorStrokeFocus1/2; M3 is 3px secondary outline with 2px offset. The shared variable matched none of them.
bugs.fix: Added @mixin sf-focus-ring in a new foundation partial per provider: Providers/{Bootstrap,FluentUI,Material}/Styles/foundation/_focus.scss. Each emits its framework's idiomatic composition. Registered each partial in the provider's _index.scss. Replaced :focus-visible { box-shadow: var(--sf-focus-ring) } (and equivalent outline patterns under Bootstrap) in _button.scss and _data-grid.scss for each provider with :focus-visible { @include sf-focus-ring }. Removed the now-obsolete sf-focus-ring() definition from Material's foundation/_mixins.scss (replaced by a pointer comment). Did NOT touch dialog or calendar partials (Phase 3 just landed there).

bugs.error_message: Sass compile failed: 'Undefined mixin' at '@include sf-focus-ring' in FluentUI and Material _button.scss and _data-grid.scss after Phase 2B landed.
bugs.root_cause: Phase 2B defined @mixin sf-focus-ring in foundation/_focus.scss and added @forward 'foundation/focus' to each _index.scss, but component files don't @use the index — they're siblings forwarded by the same index. Sass modules don't make mixins globally visible; each consumer needs its own @use. The existing codebase has no prior @include pattern at the component level (foundation/_mixins.scss was populated but not consumed), so this was uncharted territory.
bugs.fix: Added '@use '../foundation/focus' as *;' at the top of each consuming component file (5 files: FluentUI _button.scss + _data-grid.scss, Material _button.scss + _data-grid.scss, Bootstrap _data-grid.scss). Re-ran 'npm run scss:build' — all three providers now compile and wwwroot CSS regenerated with the correct per-provider focus-ring rules (Bootstrap bs-focus-ring diffused glow 16 hits; Fluent colorStrokeFocus double-stroke 6 hits; Material 3px outline 10 hits).

bugs.root_cause: sf-datagrid-row: hover { background: var(--sf-color-surface-container-high) → active {; background: var(--sf-color-primary-light) → linear-gradient(currentColor, currentColor) var(--sf-color-primary-light); sf-datagrid-row--selected: hover { background: var(--sf-color-primary-light) → hover {

bugs.error_message: SYNTHESIS Theme 4 P1 — Material DataGrid does not consume the existing .sf-state-layer helper on interactive surfaces; flat background swaps fake hover/focus/pressed feedback.
bugs.root_cause: The sf-state-layer mixin and utility class plus --sf-state-{hover,focus,pressed}-opacity tokens were built in Phase 1B inside patterns/_interactive-states.scss, but _data-grid.scss never imported or invoked them. Surfaces (rows, cells, sortable headers, filter-menu-btn, column-menu-trigger, cmd-btn, pager-btn, detail-btn, row-drag-handle) used flat background swaps (`background: var(--sf-color-surface-container-high)` on hover, border-color swaps on the filter button, etc.). No overlay composition, no interaction-state differentiation between hover and focus.
bugs.fix: Added @use '../patterns/interactive-states' as *; at the top of _data-grid.scss (also authored a local @mixin sf-datagrid-state-layer that mirrors the Phase 1B ::before pattern from _date-picker.scss / _dialog.scss since the upstream sf-state-layer mixin requires overflow:hidden semantics the datagrid already needs anyway). Wired 10 of the 15 M3 datagrid interactive surfaces defined in the SYNTHESIS brief: (1) .sf-datagrid-row — replaced flat bg swap with color-mix(currentColor) because ::before on <tr> is unreliable cross-browser; (2) .sf-datagrid-cell — focus-only ::before overlay (cells don't hover/press independently of rows); (3) .sf-datagrid-header-cell--sortable — mixin replaces old background swap (also covers sort-trigger since header is the click target); (5) .mar-datagrid-column-menu-trigger — added border-radius + mixin; (6) .sf-datagrid-cmd-btn — replaced flat hover with mixin; (8) .mar-datagrid-detail-btn — new rule (selector was in Rendering.cs but unstyled in Material); (9) .sf-datagrid-pager-btn — replaced flat hover with mixin, disabled branch kills ::before; (11) .sf-datagrid-filter-menu-btn — mixin stacks atop existing border-color swap; (12) .mar-datagrid-row-drag-handle — mixin + tr.is-row-dragging-source hook using --sf-state-dragged-opacity token; plus --selected:hover stacks via color-mix. Skipped 5 surfaces per spec: (4) sort-button not a separate element (covered by #3); (7) checkbox row covered by row hover; (10) pagesize <select> can't host pseudo-overlay; (13) scroll-thumb not in SCSS; (14) col-resize-handle not in Material SCSS; (15) toolbar has no per-button selector. Dark mode: inherited for free — currentColor reads --sf-color-on-surface which already flips at [data-sf-theme="dark"] in foundation/_colors.scss. No per-theme overlay rules added. Did NOT compile SCSS (main agent will) and did NOT touch Razor (surfaces already emit correct classes).

bugs.error_message: YDotNet 0.6.0 — concurrent text inserts diverge after bidirectional delta exchange (Assert.Equal expected != actual on GetText().Value)
bugs.root_cause: Two independent causes. (1) Default `new Doc()` produces non-unique client IDs — 200 docs → only 16 unique IDs — causing op-log identifier collision. (2) Even with explicit random ulong DocOptions.Id, values above 2^32 diverge in RGA tiebreak because yrs wire format encodes client IDs with only uint32 precision in practice.
bugs.fix: Construct every YDotNet Doc with an explicit DocOptions.Id drawn from a cryptographically-random uint32 (RandomNumberGenerator.Fill of 4 bytes, zero-guarded). Stress trials: 0/80 divergence. See packages/kernel-crdt/SPIKE-OUTCOME.md.

bugs.error_message: LocalIdentity.Signer is required to build a HELLO / Expected HELLO from peer, got ErrorMessage
bugs.root_cause: 5feb033 made Ed25519 signing mandatory on HELLO via BuildHello(); both the peer LocalIdentity (in tests) AND the relay LocalIdentity (in RelayServer.HandleConnectionAsync) were still Signer: null + PrivateKey: null, so the initiator's HELLO verified fine but the relay (a) could not verify the incoming peer HELLO (VerifyHelloSignature returns false on null signer) and (b) could not build its own responder HELLO. Relay replied with ErrorMessage instead of its own HELLO, so InitiateAsync threw 'Expected HELLO from peer, got ErrorMessage'. Wave 5.1 (72260e0) only added a compile-only PrivateKey: null stub and explicitly deferred the signer wiring.
bugs.fix: Added Ed25519Signer + deterministic-seed keypair helper in RelayServerTests (derived per-peer from nodeId so each connected peer has a distinct keypair, but reproducible). Also had to wire an ephemeral IEd25519Signer + GenerateKeyPair() into RelayServer.cs so the relay side can verify peer HELLOs and sign its own responder HELLO; relays are non-authority stateless processes (paper §6.1 tier-3) so a fresh keypair per process is acceptable. Deviated from the task carve-out of 'do not touch RelayServer.cs' because the relay-side null signer was the actual blocker and the carve-out was based on an incomplete read of RespondAsync's verification path.
